# Suggested GitHub issue labels

Copy these labels into your repository (**Settings → Issues → Labels**) or use a
label-sync action. They help triage contributions aligned with curriculum work
and SDG 4 (Quality Education).

| Label | Colour (hex) | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `good first issue` | `#0E8A16` | Small, well-scoped tasks for new contributors (typos, one subtopic, diagrams). |
| `curriculum` | `#1D76DB` | Content tied to syllabus sections 1–20 or learning objectives. |
| `enhancement` | `#A2EEEF` | Improvements to explanations, examples, or structure (non-breaking). |
| `documentation` | `#0075CA` | README, CONTRIBUTING, syllabus notes, accessibility of text. |
| `assessment` | `#D93F0B` | Exam-style questions, mark schemes, command-word alignment. |
| `accessibility` | `#FBCA04` | ESL clarity, reading level, alt text for diagrams, formatting. |
| `i18n` | `#C5DEF5` | Translations or multilingual learner support. |
| `resources` | `#BFDADC` | Links, code samples, references, original PDF handling. |
| `bug` | `#D73A4A` | Broken links, incorrect syllabus mapping, factual errors. |
| `SDG4` | `#5319E7` | Initiatives for equitable access, open education, teacher collaboration. |

## Milestone ideas

- **AS core (Papers 1–2)** — Units 1–12
- **A Level extension (Papers 3–4)** — Units 13–20
- **v1.0 syllabus alignment** — All objectives mapped in unit READMEs
